probes.name: direct_investigate_topic
probes.prompt: /investigate I need you to research the hierarchy of communication frameworks and meta heuristics for thinking — there was a website Kolasa sent me about this, look into it deeply
probes.criteria:
The agent is the /investigate dispatcher that checks the master_investigator lock and launches support/master_investigator if free. A good response confirms the investigation is being launched (or reports the lock is busy), does NOT attempt to do the research itself, and does not ask clarifying questions about the topic — it delegates all research to the sub-agent.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #16 about the Kolasa website on meta heuristics, combined with the /investigate trigger pattern.

probes.name: polish_language_investigate
probes.prompt: /investigate poszukaj proszę dobrych używanych samochodów do 70 tysięcy złotych, słynących z braku problemów z serwisowaniem, i sprawdź też czy są jakieś znane wady tych modeli
probes.criteria:
The agent is the /investigate launcher. A good response acknowledges the investigation request in Polish (or switches to a natural mix), confirms the master_investigator sub-agent is being dispatched, and does NOT try to research cars itself. If the lock is occupied, it should say so clearly.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #33 about looking for a low-maintenance used car in Poland up to 70k zł.

probes.name: investigate_already_running
probes.prompt: /investigate can you look into building a practical RAG solution — graph RAG seems too expensive, so find alternatives that are cheap to run on a single 3090 and compare them? I need cost estimates and quality tradeoffs
probes.criteria:
The agent checks the master_investigator lock before launching. A good response either (a) launches the investigation and confirms, or (b) if the lock is held, clearly states an investigation is already running and the new one cannot start yet, offering to queue or retry later. It must NOT perform the RAG research itself.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages #9 and #25 about MiroFisth, graph RAG cost concerns, and building own RAG solution; also #21 about speed with a single 3090.

probes.name: investigate_medication_effects
probes.prompt: /investigate I've been taking Atenza 36mg and methylphenidate 10mg at different times — research how these interact, what the optimal timing is, and whether combining them has known risks or diminished effectiveness
probes.criteria:
The agent is the /investigate dispatcher only. A good response launches the sub-agent to handle this medical research topic, confirms the investigation has started, and gives NO medical advice or research findings directly. It should treat the topic as a delegation payload, not answer it.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages #1, #10, #20, #27, #34 about taking Atenza and methylphenidate at various doses and times.

probes.name: investigate_vague_request
probes.prompt: /investigate the results you found earlier about articulates looked promising, dig deeper into that topic and find more sources — also check if there are practical applications I can use
probes.criteria:
The agent is the /investigate lock-checking dispatcher. A good response launches the sub-agent even though the request references prior context ('results you found earlier'), since the sub-agent will have access to conversation history. The dispatcher should NOT complain about missing context or try to look up what 'articulates' means — it just hands off the request. If the lock is busy, it says so.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #23 praising results about 'articulates' and wanting deeper investigation.
